Skip to main content Save the World Global Health & Disease

Preventable and treatable diseases kill millions every year. Cancer kills 10 million annually. Malaria, tuberculosis, and HIV remain massive killers in low-income countries.

The gap between what medicine can do and what patients can access is one of the most tragic inequities in the world today.
10 million

people die from cancer each year
What Actually Works Vaccine Infrastructure

COVID proved the model works: mRNA technology can be developed rapidly and manufactured at scale. Investing in permanent vaccine development and distribution infrastructure saves millions of lives per dollar spent.
Universal Health Coverage

Expanding health coverage to underserved populations is the single most impactful systemic intervention. Thailand and Rwanda have shown it's possible even in resource-constrained settings.
Global Fund for AIDS, TB, Malaria

One of the most cost-effective health interventions ever created. Has saved an estimated 59 million lives since 2002. Every dollar invested returns $31 in health gains and economic returns.
Cancer Early Detection

Survival rates for most cancers are dramatically higher when caught early. Expanding screening programs in low- and middle-income countries saves lives at relatively low cost.
Antimicrobial Resistance Prevention

AMR could kill 10 million people annually by 2050 if left unchecked. Investment in new antibiotics, stewardship programs, and surveillance is urgently needed.
How to Actually Do It
  1. Advocate for TRIPS waivers to enable generic medicine production
  2. Fund Gavi, the Vaccine Alliance
  3. Support AMR research legislation
  4. Push for universal health coverage in development policy
  5. Donate to MSF and the Global Fund
 What Digital Makers Can Do

You design and build digital things. Here's how those skills apply.

 Individual

Build accessible health information interfaces. Stop designing health apps that only work for affluent, English-speaking users with the latest devices. Advocate for plain-language health content and inclusive design in every health product you touch.

 Community

Volunteer your skills for local health clinics and community health organizations. Build appointment scheduling tools, patient education materials, and telemedicine interfaces for under-resourced providers.

 Country

Build open-source health data dashboards that make public health funding transparent. Design tools that help people navigate complex healthcare systems — insurance comparisons, provider directories, prescription cost trackers.

 World

Contribute to open-source health platforms used by WHO, MSF, and partners in low-resource settings. Build offline-capable health worker tools for areas with unreliable connectivity. Design interfaces for disease surveillance systems that work across languages and literacy levels.

 Further Reading Global Fund

Partnership to end AIDS, tuberculosis, and malaria.

 Gavi, the Vaccine Alliance

Saving lives and protecting health by increasing access to immunisation.

 Médecins Sans Frontières

Medical humanitarian assistance where it's needed most.

 World Health Organization

Directing and coordinating international health within the United Nations.

Built with the Eddie Design System by Brad Frost.
